premise: After my husband died, I poured $175,000 of my pension into buying a duplex for my son and his wife with the promise they’d pay me rent and eventually buy me out — they forged my signature on a quitclaim deed, sold the building for $200,000, and disappeared. Seven years later I spotted their new four-bedroom house on social media, proved the fraud in court, and not only reclaimed every cent with interest but also foreclosed on that house and turned it into a vacation rental that now funds my retirement.
